lead: Post-close 2026-07-21: memory-chip rally breaks the 7,504 wall while Brent closes above Monday's high — stocks and oil tell two different war stories

sources.use: FETCHED. US bombed southern-Iran targets; Iran struck US sites in Bahrain/Kuwait/Jordan; Hormuz tanker hit by unknown projectile, crew abandoned ship; Houthis threatened naval blockade of Saudi Arabia; two Saudi tankers reversed in Red Sea; mediators floated 10-day ceasefire. UBS Staunovo + KCM Waterer quotes. Shot: visuals/02-bnn-oil.mp4.

FETCHED. Memory-led rally: Micron +12%, Sandisk +14%, AMD +8% on strong South Korean export data signaling AI demand; indexes snap three-day losing streak (Nasdaq +1.29%, S&P +0.89%, Dow +0.74%). Shot: visuals/09-fool-memory.mp4.

FETCHED. Danaher beat (adj EPS $1.94 vs $1.83, rev $6.27B +5.5%) but cut 2026 core growth to 3-4% from 3-6%, Q3 2-3%, bioprocessing outlook lowered; biggest S&P decliner. Shot: visuals/10-benzinga-dhr.mp4.
FETCHED. Nvidia regulatory filing shows 9.3% stake in Nebius; NBIS +19% (~$217, +160% YTD), CoreWeave +9%; builds on $2B March-2025 investment.
FETCHED. Semis surged ahead of big-tech earnings, Alphabet reports Wednesday; oil highest since mid-June; Monday's 50% Canada tariff raft as overhang; NBIS +18.78%, SMCI +7.01% closes.
FETCHED. 3M adj EPS $2.40, revenue $6.5B, organic +5.4%, FY adj-EPS guide raised to $8.80-8.95 from $8.50-8.70; CEO Brown margin quote.
FETCHED. GM adj EPS $3.57 vs $3.18 est, rev $48B, FY adj-EBIT guide to $14-16B (second raise), shares +4.5%; guide assumes $2.5-3.5B gross tariff costs.
FOMC meeting July 28-29 (calendar fact; carried from 07-20 pack).

unverified_banned: CNBC ~88%-of-reporters-beat breadth stat (FactSet via CNBC) — fetch 403, SNIPPET only; banned from on-air attribution
3M intraday share move (+7-9%) — move itself snippet-sourced; 3M numbers/guide are release-sourced and usable; the MOVE is not spoken with a figure
Warsh hike-debate framing (Bloomberg newsletter Jul 14, TechTimes Jul 15) — pre-dated context, not a Jul-21 fact; FOMC spoken as calendar date only
Web closing prints for indexes/oil/yields — TradingView feed is SoT for levels per doctrine
